one_liner: Slack provides a broad, self-serve REST-style Web API secured via OAuth2/bearer tokens, with existing community MCP servers making agent toolkits buildable today.

auth_methods.notes: Standard OAuth 2.0 flow (oauth.v2.access) to obtain bearer tokens scoped to a workspace; recommended approach for third-party apps.
Bearer tokens (including test/dev tokens) passed via Authorization header or token param; bot/user tokens generated via app configuration.

self_serve_notes: Developers can create a Slack app and generate OAuth tokens for free on the Free plan to build/test integrations; some capabilities depend on the host workspace's plan (e.g., 10-app limit, 90-day history) and commercial distribution may require additional agreements/review.

api_surface.notes: Slack Web API is a broad RPC-style HTTP API with hundreds of methods for messaging, channels, users, files, etc. Also has Events API, RTM, and SDKs.

main_blocker: None significant; official OAuth-based apps may require admin approval for installation in some workspaces, and some community MCP servers rely on session tokens which are against Slack's ToS.

evidence.supports: Describes Web API as broad REST-like RPC API
Endpoint to check workspace plan programmatically

flags: Community MCP servers using session tokens violate Slack ToS
Official OAuth apps may need workspace admin approval for installation
